name: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **IRGC FACTIONAL POSITIONING POST-MAY 5 PAUSE: TASNIM MAINTAINED UAE PRESSURE BUT DID NOT ESCALATE VS US AFTER PAUSE; HARDLINE KAYHAN COLUMN THREATENED EUROPEAN BASES AS 'LEGITIMATE TARGETS.'** Internal Iranian posture divergence is now structural intelligence: Pezeshkian/Araghchi civilian-government track validated by the May 5 pause-with-progress framework; IRGC/hardliners (Tasnim aggressive on UAE; Kayhan hardline column on Europe) maintaining leverage rhetoric against secondary actors (UAE, Europe). The May 4 multi-axis kinetic restart (12 ballistic + 3 cruise + 4 drones at UAE + small boats at US Navy + SK HMM Namu damage) demonstrated IRGC saturation-strike capability; UAE+US air defense neutralized 18 of 19 (~95% intercept rate). The kinetic operation served as LEVERAGE that produced the diplomatic re-opening within 24 hours — once Iran fired, Trump used the cover to return to the Pakistan track. Important: the Kayhan European-bases column is HARDLINER publication, NOT government policy; no European foreign ministry escalated in response. The IRGC's coordinated saturation strike capability is the highest-stakes operational signal of the crisis arc — confirms Plan B remains on the shelf for re-activation if the May 5 pause framework fractures. The 'IRGC has overridden civilian government' framing from Apr 28-29 is REFINED: not override but FACTIONAL bifurcation with both tracks operating in parallel, civilian government on negotiating leverage, IRGC on kinetic leverage.
